statements.sql: select count(*) as aggregate from "orders" where "orders"."deleted_at" is null
select count(*) as aggregate from "orders" where "status" = 'pending' and "orders"."deleted_at" is null
select count(*) as aggregate from "orders" where "status" = 'processing' and "orders"."deleted_at" is null
select count(*) as aggregate from "orders" where "status" = 'completed' and "orders"."deleted_at" is null
select count(*) as aggregate from "orders" where "status" = 'failed' and "orders"."deleted_at" is null
select count(*) as aggregate from "orders" where "status" = 'cancelled' and "orders"."deleted_at" is null
select count(*) as aggregate from "orders" where "status" = 'refunded' and "orders"."deleted_at" is null
select count(*) as aggregate from "orders" where ("orders"."deleted_at" is null)
select * from "orders" where ("orders"."deleted_at" is null) order by "created_at" desc, "orders"."id" desc limit 10 offset 0
